metadata.title: What the Floor Hides
metadata.setting: A small, quiet town where a young man takes a weekend job clearing out a long-abandoned house and uncovers a fifty-year-old secret.

characters.name: Luca Marr
characters.description: A 19-year-old with a lean build and dark hair who is restless and always needs to know the answer to things.
characters.flaw: Too curious — he cannot leave an unexplained thing alone, even when he should.
characters.current_state: calm and resolved; has returned the metal box to the Cole family's granddaughter and is walking toward the bus stop in Fenwick
key_items: handwritten note (Cole family left Harden July 1974; E. Ward)
paper with Ray Brennan's Cale Street office address (written by Agnes Ward)
paper with Cole family granddaughter's address in Fenwick (given by Ray Brennan)

characters.name: Ray Brennan
characters.description: A cautious, businesslike man in his fifties who owns the old house and hired Luca to clear it out.
characters.flaw: Secretive — he gives information only when he must.
characters.current_state: defeated and resigned; surrendered the metal box and document to Luca under pressure; facing consequences

characters.name: Agnes Ward
characters.description: An elderly woman with white hair and pale eyes; widow of Edward Ward, living at 11 Birch Street in Harden. She knew her husband's secret for two years and said nothing.
characters.flaw: Kept silent about the secret for two years out of loyalty to her late husband.
characters.current_state: exhausted and relieved; her confession led to the secret's resolution; consequences approaching

locations.name: The Cole House
locations.description: A two-storey abandoned house on the edge of Harden, with dusty rooms, broken windows, and dark wooden floorboards.

locations.name: Harden Town
locations.description: A small, quiet town with a public library, a few old shops, and houses that have stood for over a hundred years.

locations.name: Luca's Flat
locations.description: A small rented room above a hardware shop in Harden, where Luca lives alone.

locations.name: Harden Public Library
locations.description: A cool, quiet building at the far end of Harden's main street, smelling of old paper, where old town newspapers and property records are kept.

locations.name: Agnes Ward's House
locations.description: A house at 11 Birch Street in Harden, the home of Agnes Ward, widow of Edward Ward.

locations.name: Ray Brennan's Cale Street Office
locations.description: A small office at number fourteen on Cale Street in Harden, above a hardware shop, where Ray Brennan conducts his business.

locations.name: Fenwick
locations.description: A small town with one long road and grey stone walls, where the Cole family's granddaughter lives in a low building with a brown door and a small garden.

episode_history.summary: Ray Brennan meets Luca at the Cole House, hands him a key, and tells him firmly to bag and throw away everything inside, refusing to name the family who lived there or answer any questions. While clearing the back room, Luca finds a small video camera hidden under a pile of dusty curtains; he takes it home, charges it via a cable, and plays the tape. The tape shows a man and a frightened woman hiding a metal box under a loose floorboard in a room Luca recognises as the Cole House, and the episode ends with Luca picking up the house key.

Luca returns to the Cole House and methodically searches each room until he finds the one matching the tape: pale walls, window on the left, and dark wooden floorboards. He lifts a loose board near the far wall and pulls out a small, locked metal box with the name 'COLE' scratched into its side. Ray Brennan calls that same morning and asks, too casually, whether Luca found anything of value; Luca lies and says no, then takes the box back to his flat, where he concludes that Ray's timing was not a coincidence.

Luca fails to open the locked box with a knife and goes to the public library, where he finds a 1974 newspaper article confirming the Cole family left Harden without warning and a property record listing 'E. Ward' as a witness to the subsequent house sale; he writes both details down. When he returns to his flat that evening, the front door is ajar, a drawer has been disturbed, and the metal box is gone. Luca concludes that Ray Brennan — the only person who knew about the box — is responsible for the theft.

The morning after the theft, Luca returns to the library and asks the librarian to search for the name E. Ward. She finds that Edward Ward died two years ago and that his wife, Agnes Ward, still lives in Harden at 11 Birch Street; she writes the address on a slip of paper and gives it to Luca. Luca thanks her, walks out of the library, and heads toward Birch Street, which is ten minutes away.

Luca arrives at Agnes Ward's house on Birch Street and shows her the video camera; she recognises Edward Ward on the tape and confesses that fifty years ago her husband borrowed a large sum of money from the Cole family, signed a document promising to repay it, then used his connections to force the family out of Harden to escape the debt. Agnes confirms that the metal box holds the signed document and part of the original money, that Ray Brennan knew everything, and that Ray came to her door two days ago claiming it was 'not a problem.' Agnes also admits she herself has kept silent for two years; she gives Luca a paper with Ray's Cale Street office address, and Luca leaves walking fast.

Luca arrives at Ray Brennan's Cale Street office and finds Ray about to burn the document from the metal box; Luca uses the video camera tape as leverage, forcing Ray to hand over the box. Ray also produces an address for the Cole family's granddaughter in Fenwick, which he admits to having kept for fifty years. Luca travels to Fenwick and returns the metal box — containing the signed document and part of the original money — to the granddaughter, closing the fifty-year-old secret.

series_plan.central_question: What secret did the family hide in the metal box fifty years ago, and who in Harden is protecting that secret today?

arc_beats.beat: Luca finds an old video camera in the abandoned house; the tape inside shows the previous family hiding a locked metal box under the floorboards of the very room he is standing in.

Luca lifts the floorboard and finds the real metal box, but it is locked; he also sees a name scratched on the box — Cole; his employer Ray Brennan calls and asks, too casually, whether Luca found anything of value in the house.
At the town library Luca learns the Cole family left Harden suddenly in 1974 and never came back; when he returns to his flat that evening, the metal box has been stolen.
Luca finds evidence pointing to an elderly woman, Mrs. Ward, whose late husband was a close friend of Ray Brennan and once did business with the Cole family; Luca goes to her house.

Mrs. Ward admits the truth: fifty years ago her husband took money that belonged to the Cole family, forcing them to leave town; the box holds a signed document and the cash that prove it; Brennan knew and stayed silent.
Luca locates the Cole family's descendants and returns the box to them; Mrs. Ward and Brennan face the consequences of the secret; the central question is answered and all threads are closed.

last_scene_position: Luca is on the one long road in Fenwick (loc_007), walking back toward the bus stop after delivering the metal box to the Cole family's granddaughter at her house with the brown door and small garden. He has just placed the box — containing the signed document and part of the original money — in her hands, and she has thanked him quietly. Luca carries only the video camera; the central mystery is fully resolved and he no longer needs to know anything more. This is the final episode; all threads are closed.
